entries.definition:
provision from them, nor do I want them to feed [keep] Me.
اسْتَطْعَم ʾistaṭʿama [v. X, trans.] to ask someone for food, to seek hospitality from someone (18:77) أَتَيَا أَهْلَ قَرْيَةٍ اسْتَطْعَمَا أَهْلَهَا they came to a people of a town and approached its inhabitants for food.

إِطْعَام iṭʿām [v. n.] feeding, giving food to (90:14–15) أَوْ إِطْعَامٌ فِي يَوْمٍ ذِي مَسْغَبَةٍ يَتِيمًا ذَا مَقْرَبَةٍ or feeding, on a day of extreme hunger, an orphan of kin.

طَاعِم ṭāʿim [act. part.] one who partakes of food and/or drink; eater, would-be eater (6:145) [...] عَلَى طَاعِمٍ [...] I do not find, in what has been revealed to me anything forbidden for an eater to eat, unless it be an unslaughtered dead animal [...].

طَعَام ṭaʿām I [n.] food (25:7) يَأْكُلُ الطَّعَامَ وَيَمْشِي فِي الْأَسْوَاقِ he eats food and walks in the market-places II [v. n.] 1 the act of eating (5:96) أُحِلَّ لَكُمْ صَيْدُ الْبَحْرِ وَطَعَامُهُ permitted for you is catching seafood and eating it 2 the act of feeding someone (89:18) وَلَا تَحَاضُّونَ عَلَى طَعَامِ الْمِسْكِينِ and you do not urge one another on feeding the poor.

طَعْم ṭaʿm [n.] taste (47:15) وَأَنْهَارٌ مِنْ لَبَنٍ لَمْ يَتَغَيَّرْ طَعْمُهُ and rivers of milk whose taste had not changed, [fresh-tasting].

طَعَام ṭaʿām I [n.] food (25:7) يَأْكُلُ الطَّعَامَ وَيَمْشِي فِي الْأَسْوَاقِ he eats food and walks in the market-places II [v. n.] 1 the act of eating (5:96) أُحِلَّ لَكُمْ صَيْدُ الْبَحْرِ وَطَعَامُهُ permitted for you is catching seafood and eating it 2 the act of feeding someone (89:18) وَلَا تَحَاضُّونَ عَلَى طَعَامِ الْمِسْكِينِ and you do not urge one another on feeding the poor.

entries.definition:
ṭ-gh-w/y (of a liquid) to overflow, to transgress, to exceed the limits, to be excessive, to violate established norms, to be tyrannical, tyranny. The word طاغوت ṭāghūt, which is classified under this root, is recognised by al-Suyūṭī as a borrowing from
566
entries.definition_english:
ṭ-gh-w/y (of a liquid) to overflow, to transgress, to exceed the limits, to be excessive, to violate established norms, to be tyrannical, tyranny. The word طاغوت ṭāghūt, which is classified under this root, is recognised by al-Suyūṭī as a borrowing from
566

entries.definition:
Ethiopian, meaning كاهن kāhin (diviner, priest), while the majority of Arab philologists, however, consider it to be a genuine Arabic word. Western scholars generally regard it as a loan from either Hebrew or Aramaic. Of this root, eight forms occur 39 times in the Qurʾan: طَغَى ṭaghā 12 times; أَطْغَى ʾaṭghā (1) once; أَطْغَى ʾaṭghā (2) once; طَاغُون ṭāghūn six times; طَاغِيَة ṭāghiyatun once; طَغْوَى ṭaghwā once; طُغْيَان ṭughyān nine times and طَاغُوت ṭāghūt eight times.
طَغَى ṭaghā a [v. intrans.] 1 (of water) to overflow, to be tumultuous (69:11) إِنَّا لَمَّا طَغَى الْمَاءُ حَمَلْنَاكُمْ فِي الْجَارِيَةِ when the water flooded We carried you in the sailing vessel 2 to violate the established norms of justice (55:8) أَلَّا تَطْغَوْا فِي الْمِيزَانِ so that you do not transgress [the norms of justice] in weighing [judgement] 3 to become tyrannical (20:24) اذْهَبْ إِلَىٰ فِرْعَوْنَ إِنَّهُ طَغَى go to Pharaoh, for he has truly become tyrannical 4 to veer away, to wander off, to quit, to go off the mark (53:17) مَا زَاغَ الْبَصَرُ وَمَا طَغَى [his] sight never wavered, nor did it wander.

أَطْغَى ʾaṭghā (1) [v. IV, trans.] to cause to violate the norms of goodness (50:27) رَبَّنَا مَا أَطْغَيْتُهُ our Lord, I did not make him transgress.

أَطْغَى ʾaṭghā (2) [elat.] more/most tyrannical (53:52) كَانُوا هُمْ أَظْلَمَ وَأَطْغَى they themselves were even more unjust and more tyrannical.

طَاغُون ṭāghūn [pl. of act. part. طَاغٍ ṭāghī] those who transgress the limits in wickedness, those who behave tyrannically (37:30) بَلْ كُنْتُمْ قَوْمًا طَاغِينَ but you were people [already] exceeding the limits.

طَاغِيَة ṭāghiyatun [quasi-act. part./n.] overpowering calamity (69:5) فَأَمَّا ثَمُودُ فَأُهْلِكُوا بِالطَّاغِيَةِ as for Thamūd they were destroyed by an overpowering calamity.

طَغْوَى ṭaghwā [n.] transgression, wickedness, rebellion (91:11) كَذَّبَتْ ثَمُودُ بِطَغْوَاهَا the people of Thamūd called [their messenger] a liar, because of their transgression.

طُغْيَان ṭughyān [v. n./n.] 1 transgressing, acting wickedly;

567

Ethiopian, meaning كاهن kāhin (diviner, priest), while the majority of Arab philologists, however, consider it to be a genuine Arabic word. Western scholars generally regard it as a loan from either Hebrew or Aramaic. أَطْغَى ʾaṭghā (2) [elat.] more/most tyrannical (53:52) كَانُوا هُمْ أَظْلَمَ وَأَطْغَى they themselves were even more unjust and more tyrannical.

طَغْوَى ṭaghwā [n.] transgression, wickedness, rebellion (91:11) كَذَّبَتْ ثَمُودُ بِطَغْوَاهَا the people of Thamūd called [their messenger] a liar, because of their transgression.

طُغْيَان ṭughyān [v. n./n.] 1 transgressing, acting wickedly;

567
